refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Correlate with telemetry data (vehicle speed, acceleration, operator input latency, safety system activation events) to isolate performance bottlenecks.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2a: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation response time and travel speed within safety envelope; allocate cycle time budgets to load, travel, and dump sub-cycles; implement closed-loop control with predictive path planning.

DP-2b: Minimize teleoperation command latency via low-latency network link (5G/dedicated), edge-based command processing, and predictive operator input buffering; implement fallback to autonomous sub-cycle execution if latency exceeds threshold.

DP-2c: Design safety services to operate in parallel with primary control loop; use safety-aware motion planning to minimize safety-induced timing overhead; implement safety constraint pre-computation.

DP-2d: Instrument vehicle and teleoperation systems with cycle time telemetry (event timestamps, latency probes, safety event logging); aggregate telemetry in Safety Services and expose via Telemetry interface for KPI reporting.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2: Integrated cycle time optimization across vehicle actuation (DP-2a), teleoperation latency management (DP-2b), safety-aware motion planning (DP-2c), and real-time telemetry instrumentation (DP-2d). Cycle time is achieved through coordinated design of vehicle dynamics, network/control latency, safety constraint integration, and continuous performance monitoring.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation speed (DP-2a) is coupled to safety constraint overhead (DP-2c): aggressive acceleration may trigger safety interventions, increasing cycle time.
Teleoperation latency (DP-2b) is coupled to vehicle responsiveness (DP-2a): high latency requires slower vehicle dynamics to maintain operator control authority.
Telemetry instrumentation (DP-2d) introduces computational overhead on vehicle and safety services, potentially impacting real-time control loop timing.
Safety services (DP-2c) and vehicle control (DP-2a) share computational resources; contention may cause jitter in cycle time.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP exhibits strong coupling across vehicle dynamics, teleoperation, safety, and telemetry domains. This is inherent to cycle time optimization in a safety-critical system. Coupling is not eliminable at this level but must be managed through explicit interface contracts (latency budgets, safety overhead caps, telemetry sampling rates) and iterative design refinement.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: CT_target is achievable within the safety envelope and network latency constraints of the deployment site.
ASSUMPTION: Duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain, operator skill) is stable and representative.
RISK: If teleoperation latency exceeds latency_budget, cycle time target may be unachievable without autonomous sub-cycle execution (increases complexity and safety validation burden).
RISK: Safety constraint overhead (e.g., collision avoidance, stability monitoring) may consume a significant fraction of the cycle time budget, leaving insufficient margin for vehicle actuation.
RISK: Telemetry instrumentation overhead may introduce non-deterministic jitter in control loop timing, affecting p95 cycle time.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Decompose DP-2 into four independent sub-DPs (DP-2a, DP-2b, DP-2c, DP-2d) and assign each to a distinct implementation module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and a dedicated Telemetry/Monitoring module). Define explicit interface contracts (latency budgets, safety overhead caps, telemetry sampling rates) to manage coupling. Proceed to next decomposition level to detail each sub-DP.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level cycle time performance integration. The NGHE Solution module owns the end-to-end cycle time KPI and coordinates the Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules to achieve CT_target. Cycle time telemetry is aggregated and exposed via the Telemetry interface.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Cycle time KPI data (p95 cycle time, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety intervention events) reported to Site Manager and System Engineer for performance monitoring and root cause analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain, operator skill level) and CT_target specification from Site Manager / Project Manager.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety constraints and safety event notifications from Safety Services; cycle time impact of safety interventions reported back to Safety Services for optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Teleoperation command latency and network availability status; cycle time performance may degrade if Network Link latency exceeds budget.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration point for cycle time KPI. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) contribute to cycle time performance. Ownership of the KPI and coordination logic is naturally contained within NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry aggregation and interface coordination logic.

Vehicle owns actuation dynamics (DP-2a) and contributes significantly to cycle time. However, vehicle cycle time is constrained by teleoperation latency (DP-2b) and safety overhead (DP-2c), which are owned by other modules. Diffusion: vehicle must expose cycle time telemetry and accept latency/safety constraints from external interfaces.

Teleoperation owns command latency management (DP-2b) and is critical to cycle time. However, teleoperation latency is constrained by Network Link availability and vehicle responsiveness. Diffusion: teleoperation must coordinate with Vehicle and Network Link to manage end-to-end latency.

Safety Services owns safety-aware motion planning (DP-2c) and must minimize safety overhead impact on cycle time. Safety Services also owns telemetry instrumentation for safety events (DP-2d). Diffusion: Safety Services must coordinate with Vehicle to enforce safety constraints without excessive timing overhead.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (system-level integration) with distributed ownership of sub-DPs: Vehicle (DP-2a), Teleoperation (DP-2b), Safety Services (DP-2c and DP-2d).
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI is a system-level emergent property that cannot be fully contained within a single module. NGHE Solution owns the KPI definition, acceptance criteria, and telemetry aggregation. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services each own sub-DPs that contribute to cycle time. Coupling across modules is managed via explicit interface contracts (latency budgets, safety overhead caps, telemetry sampling rates).
containment_risks: RISK: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are designed independently without tight interface contracts, cycle time performance may be suboptimal or unpredictable.
RISK: Telemetry instrumentation (DP-2d) is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. If telemetry collection is not coordinated, data may be incomplete or inconsistent.

RISK: Cycle time optimization may require trade-offs between vehicle speed, teleoperation latency, and safety overhead. If ownership boundaries are unclear, optimization decisions may be deferred or conflicted.

RISK: Network Link latency is external to NGHE Solution and may vary with site conditions. If Network Link latency exceeds budget, cycle time target may be unachievable, but NGHE Solution has limited control over Network Link.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit interface contracts between NGHE Solution and sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services): (1) Latency budget allocation: Vehicle ≤ X ms, Teleoperation ≤ Y ms, Safety Services ≤ Z ms, with X+Y+Z ≤ CT_target. (2) Telemetry schema: Define standardized telemetry fields (cycle start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency probes, safety event flags) to be collected by each sub-module and aggregated by NGHE Solution. (3) Safety overhead cap: Safety Services shall not exceed Z ms of cycle time overhead; if safety constraint requires more time, escalate to Site Manager for duty cycle adjustment. (4) Network Link SLA: Define acceptable Network Link latency and availability; if Network Link exceeds SLA, trigger fallback to autonomous sub-cycle execution or alert Site Manager.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (DP-2: integrated cycle time optimization) is logically sound but exhibits strong coupling across vehicle dynamics, teleoperation, safety, and telemetry domains. The coupling is not eliminable at this level and must be managed through explicit interface contracts. The DP is not sufficiently decoupled to proceed directly to implementation. Recommendation: Decompose DP-2 into four independent sub-DPs (DP-2a, DP-2b, DP-2c, DP-2d) and refine each sub-DP at the next decomposition level to reduce coupling and clarify design intent.

diffusion_risks: Cycle time KPI is a system-level emergent property that spans Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. No single module fully contains the KPI; ownership is distributed.
Telemetry instrumentation (DP-2d) is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Telemetry collection and aggregation logic is diffused across multiple modules.

Cycle time optimization requires trade-offs between vehicle speed, teleoperation latency, and safety overhead. Optimization decisions may be deferred or conflicted if ownership boundaries are unclear.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate system-level integration point for cycle time KPI. Sub-DPs are assigned to Vehicle (DP-2a), Teleoperation (DP-2b), Safety Services (DP-2c and DP-2d). Coupling across modules is managed via explicit interface contracts. Telemetry aggregation logic is owned by NGHE Solution and exposed via the Telemetry interface.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI can be verified by measuring p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Telemetry data (cycle timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) can be collected from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules and aggregated by NGHE Solution. Root cause analysis of cycle time variance can be performed by correlating telemetry data with duty cycle conditions and site conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to four sub-FRs (FR-2.1 to FR-2.4) and corresponding sub-DPs (DP-2a to DP-2d). Each sub-DP is assigned to a distinct implementation module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Allocation is clear and traceable. However, cycle time is an emergent property that depends on the interaction of all sub-modules; verification requires system-level testing and integration.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1 (FR-DP Gate): Decompose DP-2 into four independent sub-DPs (DP-2a, DP-2b, DP-2c, DP-2d) and refine each sub-DP at the next decomposition level to reduce coupling and clarify design intent.

REVISION 2 (DP-IM Gate): Establish explicit interface contracts between NGHE Solution and sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) to manage coupling: (a) Latency budget allocation, (b) Telemetry schema, (c) Safety overhead cap, (d) Network Link SLA.

REVISION 3 (FR-IM Gate): Define acceptance criteria for cycle time KPI in terms of measurable telemetry data (p95 cycle time, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions). Establish telemetry collection and aggregation procedures to support verification.

decision.justification: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-002) is well-defined and operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target). The selected DP (DP-2: integrated cycle time optimization) is logically sound but exhibits strong coupling across vehicle dynamics, teleoperation, safety, and telemetry domains. The coupling is inherent to cycle time optimization in a safety-critical system and cannot be eliminated at this level. To proceed, the DP must be decomposed into four independent sub-DPs (DP-2a, DP-2b, DP-2c, DP-2d) and refined at the next decomposition level. The IM assignment (NGHE Solution with distributed ownership of sub-modules) is appropriate for system-level integration. Explicit interface contracts must be established to manage coupling across modules. Proceed to next decomposition level to detail each sub-DP and refine IM assignments for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

revise.why: The selected DP (DP-2) is coupled across vehicle dynamics, teleoperation, safety, and telemetry domains. Decompose DP-2 into four independent sub-DPs (DP-2a, DP-2b, DP-2c, DP-2d) to clarify design intent and reduce coupling. Establish explicit interface contracts (latency budgets, safety overhead caps, telemetry sampling rates) to manage coupling across modules.

assumptions: ASSUMPTION: CT_target is achievable within the safety envelope and network latency constraints of the deployment site.
ASSUMPTION: Duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain, operator skill) is stable and representative.
ASSUMPTION: Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules can be designed and optimized independently, with coupling managed via explicit interface contracts.
ASSUMPTION: Telemetry instrumentation overhead is negligible (<5% of cycle time budget) and does not introduce non-deterministic jitter in control loop timing.
ASSUMPTION: Network Link latency is predictable and can be budgeted as a fixed overhead in cycle time calculation.
open_risks: RISK: If teleoperation latency exceeds latency_budget, cycle time target may be unachievable without autonomous sub-cycle execution (increases complexity and safety validation burden).
RISK: Safety constraint overhead (e.g., collision avoidance, stability monitoring) may consume a significant fraction of the cycle time budget, leaving insufficient margin for vehicle actuation.
RISK: Telemetry instrumentation overhead may introduce non-deterministic jitter in control loop timing, affecting p95 cycle time.
RISK: Network Link latency is external to NGHE Solution and may vary with site conditions. If Network Link latency exceeds budget, cycle time target may be unachievable.

RISK: Cycle time optimization may require trade-offs between vehicle speed, teleoperation latency, and safety overhead. If ownership boundaries are unclear, optimization decisions may be deferred or conflicted.
evidence_needed: EVIDENCE: Duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain, operator skill) from Site Manager / Project Manager.
EVIDENCE: CT_target specification and acceptance criteria from Site Manager / Project Manager.
EVIDENCE: Network Link latency and availability SLA from Teleoperation module.
EVIDENCE: Safety constraint overhead budget from Safety Services module.
EVIDENCE: Vehicle actuation response time and travel speed specifications from Vehicle module.
EVIDENCE: Telemetry instrumentation overhead analysis (computational cost, jitter impact) from NGHE Solution integration team.
EVIDENCE: p95 cycle time measurement data from ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions.
